zh.Ipswich, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可易普斯維其
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner於1230年出生在英國素福爾可易普斯維其時，他的父親Chaucer18歲，母親Mrs. Chaucer14歲。他和Duce Malyn至少有1個兒子。他去世於1280年在他的家鄉享年50歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dennington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Dennington in Suffolk, recorded in the Domesday Book as Dingifetuna, from the Old English female personal name Denegifu + Old English tūn ‘settlement, enclosure’. The name may also occasionally arise from Dinnington (Yorkshire), from the Old English masculine personal name Dunna + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Malyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Malin   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): perhaps a variant of   Malin   3 or 4.

variants.name: Malin
meanings: English: from the Middle English female personal name Malin, a diminutive of Mall or Mald (Maud). See Mall and compare Mallinson and Maude 2.

Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metronymic from the Yiddish female personal name Male (a back-formation from Malka as if it contained the Slavic diminutive suffix -ke), with the addition of the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): habitational name from Malin, a place in Ukraine.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Taverner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name; from Middle English taverner ‘tavern keeper’ (Old French tavernier).

variants.name: Tavenner
meanings: English (Gloucestershire and Devon): variant of   Taverner  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner was born in 1230, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, his father, Chaucer, was 18 and his mother, Mrs. Chaucer, was 14. He had at least 1 son with Duce Malyn. He died in 1280, in his hometown, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner est né en 1230, à Ipswich, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Chaucer, avait 18 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Chaucer, avait 14 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Duce Malyn. Il est décédé en 1280, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner 1230 in Ipswich, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Chaucer 18 und seine Mutter Mrs. Chaucer 14. Er hatte mit Duce Malyn mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1280 im Alter von 50 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner è nato il nel 1230 nel Ipswich, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Chaucer, aveva 18 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Chaucer, aveva 14 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Duce Malyn. Morì nel 1280, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 50 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Tavernerが1230年にイギリススフォルクイプスウィチで生まれた時，父Chaucerは18歳，母Mrs. Chaucerは14歳であった。彼はDuce Malynとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1280年に故郷で50歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner(이)가 1230년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Chaucer(은)는 18세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Chaucer(은)는 14세였다. 그는 Duce Malyn(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1280년 그의 고향에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner nasceu em 1230, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Chaucer, tinha 18 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Chaucer, tinha 14 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Duce Malyn. Ele faleceu em 1280, em sua cidade natal, com 50 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner родился в 1230 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Chaucer было 18 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Chaucer, 14. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Duce Malyn. Он умер в 1280 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 50 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Dennington - Malyn of le Taverner nació en 1230, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Chaucer, tenía 18 años y su mamá, Mrs. Chaucer, tenía 14 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Duce Malyn. Murió en 1280, en su pueblo, a la edad de 50 años.
